openapi.name: get_weather

info.title: get weather data
info.description: Retrieves current weather data for a location based on wttr.in.

get.description: Get weather information for a specific location

parameters.name: location
parameters.description: City or location to retrieve the weather for

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Always use j1 value for this parameter

content.text: The weather in Seattle, WA today is overcast with a temperature of about 9°C (48°F). The wind is coming from the south-southwest at around 8 km/h (5 mph). The humidity is at 58%, and there is no precipitation currently. Visibility is around 16 km (9 miles).

RequestBody.instructions:
We just ran Python code and captured a Python array of print statements.
Validate whether sample execution/output is correct for a tool-driven assistant workflow.

Prefer matched/substantive data in the final output when available.

Mark `correct = false` for:
- Exceptions, stack traces, explicit error/failure messages.
- Timeout/auth/connection/service errors that prevent normal completion.
- Malformed/corrupted output indicating broken processing.
- Tool invocation failures where the sample cannot proceed as designed.

Follow-up questions are allowed, but only as supplementary behavior.
If the output mainly asks follow-up questions without providing matched/substantive data, mark false.

Important distinction:
- Empty tool payloads by themselves (for example `[]` or `""`) can be valid and should not automatically fail.
- But if the assistant explicitly states tool failure/inability (for example "unable to retrieve ..."),
    treat that as a failure signal and mark `correct = false`.

Mark `correct = true` when execution succeeds and the output includes matched/substantive data,
even if it also asks follow-up questions.

Always include `reason` with a concise explanation tied to the observed print output.

content.text: {"correct":true,"reason":"The array of print statements shows successful creation of an OpenAI client, the correct POST request to create an agent version with the tool specification, receiving a 200 OK response with detailed agent version information, performing a request to get a weather response for Seattle, receiving a 200 OK with completed status and no errors, and final agent response text containing substantive weather details for Seattle. The session concludes with a successful cleanup DELETE request to remove the agent version, also returning 200 OK and confirmation of deletion. There are no errors or timeouts. The output includes detailed, substantive data matching the requested weather information, demonstrating correct execution for the tool-driven assistant workflow."}
